entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the crop studied for its response to heat stress

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: High temperature conditions, specifically 45 °C, that impact plant physiological and biochemical properties

entities.name: HEAT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain function and productivity under high temperature stress

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The long-term shift in global temperatures affecting agricultural production

entities.name: GLOBAL FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring reliable access to sufficient and nutritious food

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINE
entities.description: A population, specifically JL 24 × 55–437, used to derive genotypes for study

entities.name: CATALASE
entities.description: An enzyme whose activity helps plants withstand oxidative burst during heat stress

entities.name: PEROXIDASE
entities.description: An enzyme whose activity helps plants withstand oxidative burst during heat stress

entities.name: MALONDIALDEHYDE LEVELS
entities.description: A biochemical marker used to indicate the extent of membrane damage

entities.name: HEAT SHOCK PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins, specifically HSP17, whose expression is associated with heat stress tolerance

entities.name: ANTIOXIDANT ACTIVITY
entities.description: The combined enzymatic and non-enzymatic activity that protects plants from oxidative stress

entities.name: MEMBRANE DAMAGE
entities.description: Physiological degradation of cell membranes caused by heat stress
